calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE
Off Calendar Feb-21-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Petitioner Jason Brown, in pro per present.

Hearing Re: RESPONDENT CALIFORNIA MASSAGE THERAPY COUNCIL's MOTION FOR CHANGE OF VENUE.

Matter discussed. The Court notes Petitioner failed to contest the tentative ruling in a timely fashion. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Respondent California Massage Therapy Council's motion to change venue to Sacramento County Superior Court is granted. Business and Professions Code 4616 provides: "The council shall be sued only in the county of its principal office, which shall be Sacramento, unless otherwise designated by the council." Petitioner cites Business and Professions Code 4610(i), which says a council decision may be challenged by an action "in a court of competent jurisdiction." However, "the cardinal rule of statutory construction" is that statutes "must be construed together, and harmonized," when at all possible. (People v. Moroney (1944) 24 Cal.2d 638, 642.) Sacramento Superior is "a court of competent jurisdiction;" section 4610(i) does not say "any court of competent jurisdiction" - language the California Legislature uses when it so intends. (See, e.g., Penal Code 777.) Petitioner shall pay the transfer costs. No attorney fees are awarded.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
